title: leionema beckleri
intro: Leionema beckleri, commonly known as the Mountain Matrush or Swamp Matrush, is a small shrub native to eastern Australia. It is a plant in the family Rutaceae, which is known for its citrus and lemon myrtle species. Research has shown that Leionema beckleri has potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and potentially reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Leionema beckleri include citral, limonene, and beta-pinene, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

By incorporating Leionema beckleri into one's lifestyle, individuals may experience a significant improvement in their overall quality of life, including improved health, increased energy levels, and enhanced mental clarity.

side_effects: Leionema beckleri can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and skin irritation. Ingesting the plant may cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged skin contact or excessive use can lead to redness, itching, and even blistering.
